Unlike the maximum subarray sum problem, a negative number here could be very useful if it's multiplied by another negative number later on.
Because of negative numbers, you need to keep track of both the maximum product so far AND the minimum (most negative) product so far.
When you encounter a negative number, the maximum product ending at that position could come from the minimum product ending at the previous position multiplied by this negative number.
You can swap the max and min when the current number is negative, then compute the new max and min using `Math.max(nums[i], max * nums[i])`.
